Title: A Conserved Network of Transcriptional Activators and Repressors Regulates Anthocyanin Pigmentation in Eudicots

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB; C-terminal EAR motif (DLNxxP-type, DLNSPP); bHLH-interaction motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Sepals; stems; pedicels; leaves; flower buds; petal veins/blush
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation associated with developmental signaling and environmental/light-stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation; RNAi increases pigmentation and anthocyanin content, while overexpression reduces or abolishes detectable sepal anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core anthocyanin repressor in petunia that directly limits anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression and suppresses the key bHLH activator AN1, providing a double-lockdown repression mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression as an MBW complex corepressor via its EAR motif; repression of both structural anthocyanin genes and regulator AN1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and MBW-network feedback control
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AN1; DFR-A; HF1-1/F3'5'H; AN6/DFR-A; ANS; RT/3RT; 5GT; AN9/GST; MYBx; MYB27
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AN1, MYBx, MYB27 promoter activity, and anthocyanin pathway gene expression; suppression lowers DFR-A, ANS, 3RT, 5GT, GST, HF1-1 transcripts when overexpressed and elevates them when silenced
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions within or bound to the MYB-bHLH-WDR (MBW) complex; interaction with activator MYBs is bridged by AN1 or JAF13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flowers during active anthocyanin accumulation; expressed in shade/light-stressed leaves; peaks during petal development before maturation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MBW activation complex; AN1 direct activation; high light/light stress; developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback repressor induced by the MBW activation complex; also represses its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light stress/high light versus shade; ambient greenhouse light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi in petunia; HPLC anthocyanin quantification; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase promoter assays; Y2H; Y3H; GFP localization; transient biolistic assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for reducing or fine-tuning anthocyanin repression to enhance pigmentation in ornamental or nutritionally pigmented tissues, but manipulation may alter spatial patterning and light-responsive regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression broadly suppresses pigmentation patterns; silencing enhances pigmentation in multiple vegetative/floral tissues and could perturb normal pattern formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB27 is an R2R3-MYB anthocyanin repressor in petunia. 2. RNAi enhances anthocyanin accumulation, whereas overexpression reduces pigmentation and eliminates detectable sepal anthocyanins by HPLC. 3. MYB27 represses both anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and the key bHLH regulator AN1. 4. MYB27 acts as an MBW-complex corepressor requiring its EAR motif. 5. MYB27 is itself activated by the MBW complex and participates in feedback repression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts cell autonomously; GFP-MYB27 signal was transient, suggesting posttranslational instability/degradation; did not measurably alter flavonol accumulation in sepals

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R3-MYB; small bHLH-binding MYB repressor lacking active repression domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell-wide localization when GFP-fused; mobile between cells in petal epidermis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation associated with developmental patterning and environmental/light-stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation by inhibiting MBW complex activity; reduces AN11 complementation-driven pigmentation in petal biolistic assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Mobile competitive inhibitor that provides feedback repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis by titrating bHLH factors and limiting formation of active MBW complexes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Competitive inhibition/squelching of MBW complex formation through bHLH binding; indirect repression of anthocyanin gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and lateral inhibition/pattern formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AN1- and JAF13-containing MBW complexes; MYBx promoter; MYB27 promoter; AN1 promoter (modest)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses activation of DFR-A, MYBx, MYB27, and modestly AN1 promoters, with stronger effects on DPL/JAF13 than DPL/AN1 complexes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with MBW complex assembly by binding bHLH partners AN1 and JAF13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in light-stressed leaves; high in Lc overexpression plants; transiently expressed at flower developmental stages 3-4; reduced in an2 background
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MBW activation complex; high light/light stress; AN1 activity; DPL/PHZ overexpression; Lc overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback repressor activated by the MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase promoter assays; Y2H; GFP localization; biolistic complementation/repression assay in an11 petals
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for tuning pigment intensity and patterning via mobile repression, but strong manipulation could disrupt desired spatial anthocyanin patterns
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce pigmentation intensity and alter intercellular patterning/halo formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYBx is an R3-MYB anthocyanin repressor in petunia. 2. It is activated by the MBW network and light-inducible anthocyanin regulators. 3. MYBx binds AN1/JAF13 and competitively inhibits MBW activity, suppressing DFR-A and regulatory promoters. 4. MYBx moves between cells and mediates non-cell-autonomous repression important for pigmentation patterning.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suppressive effect is stronger against JAF13-containing than AN1-containing complexes; mobility likely contributes to spot/halo pattern formation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH; anthocyanin regulator; MBW component; bHLH2 clade
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation in vegetative and floral tissues; environmental/light-stress and developmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator of anthocyanin pigmentation; its expression is reinforced by MBW activation and repressed by MYB27
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key bHLH component required for anthocyanin MBW activation complex and a central reinforcement node in the regulatory hierarchy
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MBW component; self/hierarchical reinforcement through activation of its own promoter by MBW complexes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthetic gene activation and MBW-network reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including DFR-A; MYB27; MYBx; AN1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin pathway genes and promotes MYB27, MYBx, and AN1 expression when in MBW complexes with activator MYBs and AN11
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of MBW activator complex with AN2/DPL/PHZ and AN11
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AN2; DPL; PHZ; AN11; MYB27; MYBx; JAF13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by high light; expressed during flower development with peak before maturation; increased in MYB27 RNAi and reduced in MYB27 OE
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MBW activators DPL/PHZ/AN2; high light; Lc overexpression; repression by MYB27
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive reinforcement/self-activation through MBW complexes; subject to feedback repression by MYB27 and competitive inhibition by MYBx
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in transgenics; promoter activation assays; Y2H/Y3H; transient complementation in an11 petals; genetic background analyses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for anthocyanin enhancement, but essential-network position means engineering may have broad effects on patterning and feedback control
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misexpression may broadly alter pigmentation patterns and regulatory balance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN1 is an essential positive bHLH regulator of petunia anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Its promoter is activated by MBW complexes and repressed by MYB27. 3. AN1 activates anthocyanin structural genes and induces feedback repressors MYB27 and MYBx. 4. MYBx competitively inhibits AN1 activity, while MYB27 suppresses AN1 transcript accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cell autonomous in petal assays; an1 mutants lack anthocyanins despite JAF13 expression

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petal epidermis; vegetative and floral pigmented tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required cofactor for anthocyanin MBW activation; complementation restores anthocyanin foci in an11 petals and demonstrates intercellular mobility
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential mobile WDR component enabling active MBW complex formation and likely contributing to pigmentation patterning through cell-to-cell movement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin gene activation and intercellular patterning support
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of anthocyanin genes through MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core MBW complex member with R2R3-MYB activators and bHLH AN1/JAF13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively stable; increased in high-light-treated Lc petunia
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; biolistic complementation in an11 mutant petals; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for restoring or modulating pigmentation where MBW complex formation is limiting, but mobility may complicate spatial engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Intercellular mobility may affect pattern boundaries
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN11 is an essential WD-repeat cofactor of the petunia anthocyanin MBW complex. 2. It interacts strongly with AN1 and JAF13. 3. AN11 moves between cells in petal epidermis. 4. Its mobility may contribute to anthocyanin pattern formation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYBs tested generally did not bind AN11 except DPL in Y2H

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petal veins; leaves; vegetative pigmented tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin biosynthesis and regulatory hierarchy; overexpression increases pigmentation and induces AN1, MYBx, and MYB27
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative activator MYB that drives MBW-mediated anthocyanin induction and activation of reinforcement/feedback nodes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as DNA-binding MYB of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and upstream activation of MBW hierarchy
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AN1, MYBx, MYB27, and DFR-A promoters, especially with AN1 or JAF13
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with AN1 or JAF13 and AN11; interaction with MYB27 bridged by bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with petal venation and leaf expression; overexpression gives dark leaf phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in hierarchical activation network; subject to repression of complex output by MYB27/MYBx
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in petunia; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase promoter assays; Y2H; Y3H
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong activator for increasing anthocyanin intensity/patterning in targeted tissues
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May trigger compensatory induction of repressors MYB27 and MYBx
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPL is an anthocyanin-activating R2R3-MYB in petunia. 2. It activates AN1, MYBx, MYB27, and DFR-A promoters as part of the MBW complex. 3. Overexpression elevates pigmentation and downstream regulator expression. 4. Its activity is repressed by MYB27 and competitively inhibited by MYBx.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative activator in promoter assays; determines venation patterning in flowers

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin pigmentation, especially light-induced vegetative pigmentation; overexpression elevates anthocyanin and induces AN1, MYBx, and MYB27
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary light-responsive MYB activator for vegetative anthocyanin induction in petunia
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MBW MYB activator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression increases AN1, MYBx, and MYB27 transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MBW activation complexes with AN1/JAF13 and AN11
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by high light; principal regulator of light-induced pigmentation; determines bud blush
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in activation of feedback repressors via MBW
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance environmentally responsive anthocyanin pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely induces repressor feedback and environment-dependent effects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHZ is a light-responsive R2R3-MYB activator of petunia anthocyanin pigmentation. 2. Overexpression increases vegetative anthocyanin accumulation. 3. PHZ elevates AN1, MYBx, and MYB27 expression within the regulatory hierarchy.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Major determinant of light-regulated bud blush and vegetative pigmentation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates floral anthocyanin pigmentation; functional AN2 background reveals MYB27 overexpression can strongly suppress petal pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major floral MYB activator whose absence in MP underlies white flowers; included as a core summarized activator within the network tested by crosses and interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MBW DNA-binding activator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AN1 and structural anthocyanin genes as MBW component
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complexes with AN1/JAF13 and AN11; interacts with MYB27 via bHLH-containing complexes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Absent/nonfunctional in MP; functional in V30 and V26 floral pigmentation backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Contributes to MBW activation of AN1 and likely repressors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic background comparisons/crosses; Y2H; promoter assay context from prior and preliminary experiments cited in paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Classic activator target for enhanced floral anthocyanin pigmentation, but output remains constrained by MYB27-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can trigger stronger feedback repression network when highly active
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN2 is a major floral R2R3-MYB activator in petunia. 2. Its functional presence in V30 enables strong petal pigmentation and reveals suppression by MYB27 overexpression. 3. AN2 is part of the conserved MBW activation network targeted by MYB27 repression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Null alleles in MP explain white flowers in the main transgenic background

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in anthocyanin regulation and promoter activation, but does not substitute for AN1; complexes containing JAF13 are particularly sensitive to MYBx repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supporting bHLH activator in the anthocyanin hierarchy, likely acting upstream or in parallel to reinforce MBW function
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MBW bHLH component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of DFR-A, AN1, MYB27, and MYBx promoters with DPL
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complexes with activator MYBs and AN11; binds MYB27 and MYBx; can bridge DPL-MYB27 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DPL; PHZ; AN2; AN11; MYB27; MYBx; AN1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively unaffected by MYB27 or Lc overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; promoter activation assays; qRT-PCR; mutant evidence discussed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target for modulating anthocyanin network output, though alone insufficient to compensate for AN1 loss
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may have weaker or context-dependent effects than AN1 and may be strongly opposed by MYBx
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAF13 is a bHLH partner within petunia MBW complexes. 2. It can support activation of anthocyanin and regulatory promoters with DPL. 3. MYBx represses JAF13-containing complexes more strongly than AN1-containing complexes. 4. JAF13 cannot compensate for loss of AN1.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely functionally specialized relative to AN1 in petunia anthocyanin regulation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic expression enhances light-induced anthocyanin pigmentation in petunia leaves and strongly elevates AN1, MYBx, and MYB27 under high light
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Experimental heterologous activator used to demonstrate hierarchical induction of petunia anthocyanin regulators by excess bHLH activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as heterologous bHLH activator
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AN1; MYBx; MYB27; AN11 indirectly associated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Elevates endogenous petunia regulator transcripts, especially under high light
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in petunia MBW-related regulatory context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: CaMV35S promoter; high light modulates downstream response rather than transgene expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces feedback repressors MYB27 and MYBx indirectly through network activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in petunia; qRT-PCR under shade/high light
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates utility of bHLH engineering to drive anthocyanin, but heterologous activation is highly context dependent and induces feedback repressors
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires inductive context for maximal effect and triggers compensatory repressors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Maize Lc enhances light-induced anthocyanin pigmentation in petunia leaves. 2. Lc elevates petunia AN1, MYBx, MYB27, and AN11 expression, especially under high light. 3. Results support a hierarchical bHLH-driven anthocyanin regulatory network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species and applied species differ: Zea mays gene tested in Petunia hybrida
